name: Pura Tirta Empul
description: Famous Hindu Balinese water temple with fresh-water purification pools.

name: Pura Mengning
description: Quiet water temple for tranquil self-purification without queues.

name: Lovina Beach
description: A tranquil strip of black sand beaches offering dolphin watching tours at sunrise.

name: Pantai Penimbangan
description: Charming beach in North Bali popular with locals, featuring food stalls and a turtle hatchery.

name: Jatiluwih Rice Terraces
description: Lush green terraced hillsides that offer a breath of fresh air and peaceful sightseeing.

name: Pura Besakih
description: The Mother Temple of Bali, a grand Hindu complex situated on the slopes of Mount Agung.

name: Tanah Lot Temple
description: Iconic sea temple perched on a dramatic rock formation in the crashing waves.

name: Uluwatu Temple
description: Spectacular clifftop temple overlooking the Indian Ocean, famous for sunset Kecak dances.

name: Tegallalang Rice Terraces
description: A scenic valley of terraced rice fields offering iconic viewpoints and walking paths.

name: Sekumpul Waterfall
description: One of Bali's most majestic waterfalls, a hidden cluster of cascades deep in the jungle.

name: Gitgit Waterfall
description: Classic tropical waterfall located in a lush green valley with easy forest walking trails.

name: Mount Batur Sunrise Trek
description: An active volcano climb popular for watching sunrise over Lake Batur and Lombok.

name: Tirta Gangga Water Palace
description: A beautiful royal garden featuring maze-like koi ponds, stone carvings, and stepping stones.

name: Lempuyang Temple
description: Famous clifftop temple complex featuring the Gate of Heaven looking toward Mount Agung.

name: Kelingking Beach
description: Stunning, world-famous cliff view shaped like a T-Rex overlooking a white sand beach.

name: Angel's Billabong
description: A natural rock infinity pool with crystal clear waters opening straight to the ocean.

name: Crystal Bay
description: A palm-fringed quiet bay famous for pristine white sand and fantastic sunset snorkeling.

name: Pantai Pandawa
description: Wide white sand beach hidden behind giant limestone cliffs with sea kayaks.

name: Campuhan Ridge Walk
description: A scenic walking path along green hillsides offering views of lush river valleys.

name: Bali Botanic Garden
description: A massive, tranquil botanic reserve featuring forest pathways, glasshouses, and giant trees.

name: Pura Ulun Danu Bratan
description: A scenic temple built on the shores of Lake Bratan, appearing to float on water.

name: Munduk Waterfall
description: A quiet, cooling forest waterfall trek surrounded by clove and coffee plantations.

name: Blue Lagoon Beach
description: A hidden white sand cove popular for diving, snorkeling, and fresh seafood stalls.

name: Bukit Asah Bugbug
description: A peaceful cliffside grassy meadow ideal for ocean-view camping and sunset picnics.

name: Bias Tugel Beach
description: A secluded, quiet pocket of white sand with turquoise tide pools and blowholes.

name: Pemuteran Bay Reef
description: Quiet coastal bay containing the world's largest Biorock artificial coral reef project.

name: Menjangan Island
description: Uninhabited nature reserve islet famous for pristine coral walls and deer sightings.

name: Banyumala Twin Waterfalls
description: A spectacular pair of hidden waterfalls tumbling down a lush, mossy cliff wall.

name: West Bali National Park
description: A protected sanctuary of savannahs, mangroves, and monsoon forests home to the Bali Starling.

name: Yeh Leh Beach
description: An offbeat beach covered in thousands of smooth boulders, perfect for sunset photography.

name: Bukit Kursi Hike
description: A green ridge hike that rewards you with spectacular views of Pemuteran Bay and quiet temples.
